I need to verify whether the garbage collector handles a valid manifest with missing referenced blobs.
Looking at the mark phase, the manifest is read successfully and its config and layers are added to the referenced set.
The main issue is whether missing child blobs should make the run fail or just be ignored.
The finding is a valid test coverage gap because no test records this broken-reference case.
Priority is low because the code behavior itself appears correct.
Let me finalize the finding as confirmed with a low priority.
Actually, I should verify whether the sweep phase might still delete something incorrectly.
Looking at the sweep loop, it only iterates blobs returned by List, so missing referenced blobs are never processed.
However, the image object can still contain broken references after the run.
The verdict is still confirmed because the test gap remains reachable.
The issue is not a functional data-loss bug, but the missing coverage is real.
Let me finalize the response with that distinction.
Wait, I need to reconsider whether missing tests count as a real review finding.
I should verify the review criteria before submitting the verdict.
Looking at the changed tests, missing manifests and corrupt manifests are covered but this valid-manifest missing-blob case is not.
The finding remains valid because this edge case can regress without a focused test.
Priority remains low because the current code path succeeds.
Therefore the final verdict should confirm the test gap and avoid overstating it as a runtime bug.
